mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, labor market deterioration triggers recession expectations that compress corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE crosses 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE stays above 8.0% for more than 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer prices indicate demand-pull inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5% annually while RSAFS growth remains below 2%, or interaction term IC is negative over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal strengthening manufacturing output that supports near-term corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the signal reverses direction when global commodity prices rise above historical 80th percentile.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand that lifts corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 500,000 units signal collapsing construction demand and weakening consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when HOUST stays below 500.0 for consecutive months, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for mortgage rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates indicate robust labor market absorption that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns when wage growth exceeds 6% annually, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price indices above 0.5% monthly trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets price in inflation expectations immediately, causing forward returns to remain positive despite CPIAUCSL spikes, or the signal fails during supply-driven inflation episodes.
